briefdescription.para: Initializes a specific XIicPs instance so that the driver is ready to use.

detaileddescription.para: Functions for general setup, in xiicps.c.

The state of the device after initialization is:
Device is disabled
Slave mode

parameterdescription.para: Reference to a structure containing information about a specific IIC device. This function initializes an InstancePtr object for a specific device specified by the contents of Config.

Device base address in the virtual memory address space. The caller is responsible for keeping the address mapping from EffectiveAddr to the device physical base address unchanged once this function is invoked. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping changes after this function is called. If address translation is not used, use ConfigPtr->BaseAddress for this parameter, passing the physical address instead.

simplesect.para: The return value is XST_SUCCESS if successful.

briefdescription.para: Checks the availability of the I2C bus.

listitem.para: TRUE if the bus is busy.
FALSE if the bus is not busy.

briefdescription.para: Aborts a transfer in progress by resetting the FIFOs.
Puts more data into the transmit FIFO.

detaileddescription.para: The number of bytes is either the expected number of bytes for this transfer or available space in FIFO, which ever is less.

simplesect.para: Number of bytes left for this instance.
This is function is shared by the master and slave.

briefdescription.para: Performs I2C bus recovery using the PS I2C controller HOLD feature.

detaileddescription.para: This API is used when the I2C controller is already in Master mode and the bus is stuck due to a slave holding SDA low. The API toggles the HOLD bit to manually generate SCL pulses, allowing the slave to release the bus.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if recovery sequence is executed. XST_FAILURE if controller is not in Master mode.

parameterdescription.para: Value to enable/disable clock stretching (SCL HOLD). 1 to Enable 0 to Disable

briefdescription.para: Number of SCL pulses for bus recovery.
HOLD toggle delay in microseconds (5000 us = 5 ms) for I2C bus recovery.

detaileddescription.para: The stub is here in case the upper layers forget to set the handler.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the upper layer callback reference.
